Alter: title. Add: date, authors 1-1. Removed parameters. Some additions/deletions were parameter name changes. | [[:en:WP:UCB|Use this bot]]. [[:en:WP:DBUG|Report bugs]]. | Suggested by Abductive | #UCB_toolbar
/* Spread and acceptance in Roman Empire */ Converted textual value to numeric with corresponding % symbol to make more consistent with other percentage styling throughout the article.

/* Catholic Church */ Inserted "of" apparently omitted or removed previously. Changed "one sixth" to decimal value. [[MOS:NUMERAL]] "Comparable values nearby one another should be all spelled out or all in figures, even if one of the numbers would normally be written differently: patients' ages were five, seven, and thirty-two or ages were 5, 7, and 32, but not ages were five, seven, and 32." [[MOS#NUMBERS]] "In general, use decimals rather than fractions for measurements... " Be consistent.
/* High and Late Middle Ages */ See [[MOS:ERA]]: "Use either the BC–AD or the BCE–CE notation consistently within the same article."
/* Trinity */ Modern English grammatical convention calls for a comma after an introductory word or phrase like "In some Early Christian sarcophagi." I added such a comma here.
/* Trinity */ The second clause of this pair is not independent of the first. It was therefore grammatically incorrect to use a comma to join the two clauses. I removed the inappropriate comma.
/* Protestantism */ The second clause of this sentence is not independent. It was therefore grammatically incorrect to use a comma to join the two clauses. I removed the inappropriate comma.
/* Churches and denominations */  Modern English grammatical convention calls for "neither" to be followed by "nor" in this sort of comparative construct.
/* High and Late Middle Ages */ Modern English grammatical convention calls for a comma separating multiple, sequential adjectives ("large" and "Isolated") applied to a single noun ("monasteries").
